enonce.texte: Match each word or phrase to its correct meaning. Draw a line from the word to the definition.

questions.texte: Match the telephone vocabulary to its definition.
questions.reponse: 1. call - d. To phone someone; 2. ring - a. The sound a phone makes; 3. answer - e. To pick up the phone; 4. hang up - b. To end a call; 5. speak - c. To talk

sousQuestions.texte: What do you say when you want to end a telephone conversation?

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct word from the box: speak, call, answer, ring, hang up, hello.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct word.
questions.reponse: 1. ring; 2. answer; 3. speak; 4. call; 5. hang up; 6. Hello

sousQuestions.texte: The phone is starting to ______. Please ______ it.
May I ______ to the manager?
I need to ______ my mother.
Please don't ______ yet. I have more to say.
When you answer the phone, you say '______'.

enonce.texte: Read the sentences. Identify which sentences are polite expressions used on the phone.

questions.texte: Choose the sentences that are polite telephone expressions.

sousQuestions.texte: Which of these is a polite way to ask to speak to someone?
What do you say when someone is not available?
What do you say to ask someone to wait?
Which of these is NOT a polite telephone expression?

enonce.texte:
Read the telephone dialogue and answer the questions.

Dialogue:

Mama: Hello?
Papa: Hello Mama, it's Papa.
Mama: Oh, hello! How are you?
Papa: I am fine. May I speak to Marie, please?
Mama: Hold on, please. I will call her.
Papa: Thank you.
Mama: Marie, your father is on the phone!
Marie: Hello, Papa!
Papa: Hello, my dear! I will come home late today.
Marie: Okay, Papa. We will wait for you. Goodbye!
Papa: Goodbye, Marie.

questions.texte: Answer the questions about the dialogue.
questions.reponse: 1. Papa called; 2. Marie; 3. He asked 'May I speak to Marie, please?'; 4. 'Hold on, please'; 5. He is coming home late

sousQuestions.texte: Who called Mama?
Who does Papa want to speak to?
What polite expression does Papa use to ask to speak to Marie?
sousQuestions.reponse: May I speak to Marie, please?

sousQuestions.texte: What does Mama say to ask Papa to wait?
What news does Papa give Marie?

enonce.texte: Imagine you need to call your teacher to ask about homework. Write a short dialogue between you and the teacher (or the person who answers). Use polite expressions.

questions.reponse:
Example:
You: Hello, may I speak to Mr. John, please?
Secretary: Hold on, please. I will check if he is available.
You: Thank you.
Teacher: Hello, this is Mr. John speaking.
You: Good morning, Mr. John. I am your student, Marie. I would like to ask about the homework.
Teacher: Of course. The homework is on page 15.
You: Thank you, Mr. John. Goodbye.
Teacher: Goodbye, Marie.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the polite expression you use to ask to speak to the teacher?
sousQuestions.reponse: May I speak to Mr. John, please?

sousQuestions.texte: What does the secretary say to ask you to wait?
How do you greet the teacher when he answers?

enonce.texte:
Listen to the following dialogue and answer the questions. (Imagine you are listening to a phone conversation.)

Dialogue:

Receptionist: Hello, Cameroon Hospital. How can I help you?
Patient: Hello, may I speak to Dr. Nkolo, please?
Receptionist: I am sorry, but Dr. Nkolo is not available right now. Can I leave a message?
Patient: Yes, please. This is Mr. Atangana. I would like to confirm my appointment for tomorrow.
Receptionist: Of course, Mr. Atangana. I will give him the message.
Patient: Thank you very much. Goodbye.
Receptionist: Goodbye, Mr. Atangana.

questions.texte: Answer the questions based on the dialogue.
questions.reponse: 1. Cameroon Hospital; 2. To speak to Dr. Nkolo; 3. The doctor is not available; 4. 'Can I leave a message?'; 5. To confirm his appointment for tomorrow

sousQuestions.texte: Where is the call to?
What is the patient's request?
Why can't the patient speak to the doctor?
What polite expression does the receptionist use to offer help?
What is the message the patient leaves?
sousQuestions.reponse: He wants to confirm his appointment for tomorrow.

enonce.texte: Imagine you are at home. Your friend calls you to ask about the homework. Your mother answers the phone and gives it to you. Write a dialogue.

questions.texte: Write a complete dialogue for this scenario.
questions.reponse:
Example:
Friend: Hello, may I speak to Marie, please?
Mother: Hold on, please. I will call her.
Mother: Marie, your friend is on the phone!
Marie: Hello!
Friend: Hello, Marie. How are you?
Marie: I am fine. How are you?
Friend: I am fine too. I need to ask about the homework.
Marie: Okay, I can help you.
Friend: Thank you, Marie. Goodbye.
Marie: Goodbye!

sousQuestions.texte: What does your mother say when she answers the phone?
sousQuestions.reponse: Hold on, please. I will call her.

sousQuestions.texte: What do you say when you pick up the phone?
What does your friend ask about?

enonce.texte: You need to call your friend to invite them to your birthday party. Write a complete telephone dialogue. Include a greeting, a request, a message, and a goodbye. Use at least 8 exchanges.

questions.texte: Write a telephone dialogue inviting a friend to a birthday party.
questions.reponse:
Example:
You: Hello, may I speak to John, please?
John's mother: Hold on, please. I will get him.
John: Hello!
You: Hello, John. This is Marie.
John: Oh, hi Marie! How are you?
You: I am fine, thank you. I would like to invite you to my birthday party on Saturday.
John: That sounds great! Where is the party?
You: At my house at 3 pm.
John: I will come. Thank you for inviting me.
You: Great! I will see you on Saturday. Goodbye!
John: Goodbye, Marie.

sousQuestions.texte: What polite expression do you use to ask to speak to John?
sousQuestions.reponse: May I speak to John, please?

sousQuestions.texte: What does John's mother say?
sousQuestions.reponse: Hold on, please. I will get him.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the main message of your call?
sousQuestions.reponse: To invite John to the birthday party.

sousQuestions.texte: What information does John ask for?
